assertions.reason: commands/mission-plan.toml is valid TOML and its prompt explicitly tells the model to use activate_skill to load the mission-planner skill for the current mission, referencing both the mission directory/ID and the working directory from state.json.
evidence: Prompt text includes: 'Use activate_skill to load the mission-planner skill'
Prompt text references mission directory ~/.gemini-mc/missions/mis_<id>/ and the workingDirectory field from state.json

assertions.reason: skills/define-worker-skills/SKILL.md has valid frontmatter and includes explicit instructions for worker agent definitions covering kind: local, valid Gemini CLI tool names, and optional model/temperature/max_turns/timeout_mins fields, plus procedural startup/work/cleanup body guidance.
evidence: Frontmatter parsed from skills/define-worker-skills/SKILL.md with name=define-worker-skills and a non-empty description
Skill text includes 'kind: local' requirement and a valid tool names section listing run_shell_command, google_web_search, glob, read_many_files, etc.
Skill text documents model, temperature, max_turns, timeout_mins, and body phases Startup/Work/Cleanup

assertions.reason: Each skills/* subdirectory currently present contains a SKILL.md file with valid YAML frontmatter, and each directory name matches the skill name declared in frontmatter.
evidence: Parsed frontmatter confirms name matches directory for both skills and each includes a non-empty description

frictions: The repository does not currently have README.md at the root, so equivalent project context was taken from GEMINI.md plus mission/testing guidance.

gemini extensions link /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension printed an interactive confirmation prompt, but with cached credentials it still completed with exit code 0; no non-interactive mission-plan invocation was required for these artifact-level assertions.
